doc: Per-state ABAWD monthly hour requirement. States not listed default to federal_default_hours (80). For workfare-formula states, the calculator computes floor(snap_max_allotment[household_size] / minimum_wage_used). Only NY has sub-state regional variation (NYC/LI vs upstate). MI/SD/GA were removed from this list in schema v2: their workfare formula only applies when the volunteer site is registered as a state workfare provider — general nonprofit volunteering defaults to 80 hours. See workfare_if_registered for those cases.

workfare_if_registered.doc: States where the workfare formula yields lower required hours, BUT only when the volunteer site is registered as a state-approved workfare/comparable-workfare/SICS provider. Tended-style general 501(c)(3) volunteering at an unregistered nonprofit gets 80 hrs in these states.

MI.requirement: Site must be approved by MI DHHS as a workfare site

SD.requirement: Site must be a DLR-approved workfare site (per SD Manual §13232)

GA.requirement: Site must be a DFCS-registered Comparable Workfare provider (Form 805)

NY.note: NY divides minimum wage by jurisdiction. The Monroe County ABAWD Volunteer Participation Record formula uses 'SNAP benefit ÷ higher of federal or state minimum wage for the recipient's area.'

ME.note: Maine 3SquaresVT-style formula uses state minimum wage indexed annually (Jan 1).

MA.note: Massachusetts Work Program Participation Record (WPPR) explicitly divides SNAP benefit by MA minimum wage.

VT.note: 3SquaresVT calculates personalized hours target at approval using state min wage (auto-indexed each Jan 1).

VA.note: Virginia SNAP Manual Part XV.A.4 explicitly uses federal $7.25, NOT Virginia's higher state minimum wage ($12.41 in 2026).

PA.note: PA matches the federal minimum wage; CAO records the figure on the Employment Development Plan.
